title: s3-WGS Protocol for Nuclei Isolation and Tn5 Preparation
author: Andrew Adey
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.beb3jaqn

steps.title: Attached is a s3 molecular layout per step
steps.instruction: Attached is a s3 molecular layout per step. As well as oligonucleotides used in the protocol. Prior to starting, iTSM plate should be prepared following the protocols listed within this step. s3_oligos.xlsx Tabs with oligonucleotides to be ordered and prepared: iTSM sci_WG A14_ME_LNA PCR_i5 primers PCR_i7 primers

steps.title: For generation of Tn5 enzyme
steps.instruction: For generation of Tn5 enzyme. Generation and Purification of pTXB1.Tn5 by Andrew Adey

steps.title: For loading Tn5 enzyme
steps.instruction: For loading Tn5 enzyme . Load only a single oligonucleotide + mosaic end reverse compliment oligonucleotide per well. Load to a final concentration of equimolar 2.5uM oligonucleotide:mosaic end reverse compliment with 2.5uM Tn5. Loading Tn5 Enzyme with sci-protocol Oligonucleotides by Andrew Adey

steps.title: Construct 50mL Nuclei Isolation Buffer (NIB-Tris)
steps.instruction: Construct 50mL Nuclei Isolation Buffer (NIB-Tris): %s %s Final ConcentrationStock ConcentrationVolume of Stock 10 mM Tris HCl, pH 7.51M Tris HCl, pH7.5500 uL 10 mM NaCl5M NaCl100 uL 3mM MgCl21M MgCl2150 uL 0.1 % Igepal10% Igepal500 uL 0.1 % Tween10% Tween500 uL ddH20 dd OPTIONAL: To prevent protease degradation, we also add 2 tables of Pierce Preotease Inhibitor Tablets, EDTA-Free to NIB following construction. We then vortex to fully dissolve tablets. NIB is stable at 4 °C for at least 1 month without noticable degradation in library quality or nuclei dissociation ability. Store NIB on ice throughout nuclei dissociation and preparation of tagmentation plates.

substeps.instruction: Construct 50mL Nuclei Isolation Buffer (NIB-Tris): %s %s Final ConcentrationStock ConcentrationVolume of Stock 10 mM Tris HCl, pH 7.51M Tris HCl, pH
uL 10 mM NaCl5M NaCl100 uL 3mM MgCl21M MgCl2150 uL

% Tween10% Tween500 uL ddH20 dd OPTIONAL: To prevent protease degradation, we also add 2 tables of Pierce Preotease Inhibitor Tablets, EDTA-Free to NIB following construction. We then vortex to fully dissolve tablets. NIB is stable at 4 °C for at least 1 month without noticable degradation in library quality or nuclei dissociation ability. Store NIB on ice throughout nuclei dissociation and preparation of tagmentation plates.

steps.title: Construct 50mL Nuclei Isolation Buffer (NIB-HEPES)
steps.instruction: Construct 50mL Nuclei Isolation Buffer (NIB-HEPES): %s %s Final ConcentrationStock ConcentrationVolume of Stock 10 mM Tris HCl, pH 7.51M Tris HCl, pH7.5500 uL 10 mM NaCl5M NaCl100 uL 3mM MgCl21M MgCl2150 uL 0.1 % Igepal10% Igepal500 uL 0.1 % Tween10% Tween500 uL ddH20 dd OPTIONAL: To prevent protease degradation, we also add 2 tables of Pierce Preotease Inhibitor Tablets, EDTA-Free to NIB following construction. We then vortex to fully dissolve tablets. NIB is stable at 4 °C for at least 1 month without noticable degradation in library quality or nuclei dissociation ability. Store NIB on ice throughout nuclei dissociation and preparation of tagmentation plates.

substeps.instruction: Construct 50mL Nuclei Isolation Buffer (NIB-HEPES): %s %s Final ConcentrationStock ConcentrationVolume of Stock 10 mM Tris HCl, pH 7.51M Tris HCl, pH
uL 10 mM NaCl5M NaCl100 uL 3mM MgCl21M MgCl2150 uL

% Tween10% Tween500 uL ddH20 dd OPTIONAL: To prevent protease degradation, we also add 2 tables of Pierce Preotease Inhibitor Tablets, EDTA-Free to NIB following construction. We then vortex to fully dissolve tablets. NIB is stable at 4 °C for at least 1 month without noticable degradation in library quality or nuclei dissociation ability. Store NIB on ice throughout nuclei dissociation and preparation of tagmentation plates.

steps.title: Different isolation techniques are h ighlighted...
steps.instruction: Different isolation techniques are h ighlighted across the s3 protocol family and can be used interchangeably for downstream processing. For s3WGS we primarliy used adherent cultured cell lines. Note Isolation of nuclei is dependent on the sample being used. And optimization should be performed. Below is an example of a nuclei isolation protocol to act as general use for primary brain tissue samples. Other tissues should follow a similar dounce homogenization protocol.

steps.title: Plate 1 million cells in T25...
steps.instruction: Plate 1 million cells in T25 flask the day prior and harvest while cells are subconfluent.

steps.title: Wash adherent cell lines Aspirate off...
steps.instruction: Wash adherent cell lines Aspirate off media from flask Washed cells in flask with 5 mL, ice cold 1X PBS , and aspirate off

steps.title: Trypsinize cells
steps.instruction: Trypsinize cells: Add 5 mL, TrypLE to flask an incubate at 37 °C for 00:15:00 Collect cells and move to 15mL tube on ice.

steps.title: Spin down nuclei solution at at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Spin down nuclei solution at at 4 °C .

steps.title: Resuspend pellet in 1 mL NIB:HEPES
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet in 1 mL NIB:HEPES . Let nuclei incubate for 00:10:00 On ice

steps.title: Repeat the spin down and resuspension (Repeat step 6
steps.instruction: Repeat the spin down and resuspension (Repeat step 6.4-6.5) for a second wash.

steps.title: Resuspend cells in 300 µL, NIB:HEPES and hold on ice
steps.instruction: Resuspend cells in 300 µL, NIB:HEPES and hold on ice.

steps.title: Take aliquot of sample and dilute...
steps.instruction: Take aliquot of sample and dilute (10uL of nuclei suspension in 90 µL, NIB:HEPES for a 1:10 dilution).

steps.title: Determine nuclei/uL concentration of diluted aliquot...
steps.instruction: Determine nuclei/uL concentration of diluted aliquot through Hemocytometer or BioRad TC20 Automated Cell Counter using manufacturer's recommended protocols. Adjust calculated concentration for the stock nuclei suspension. Example TC20 image of isolated nuclei.

steps.title: Separate nuclei into 1 million nuclei...
steps.instruction: Separate nuclei into 1 million nuclei aliquots and transfer into 5mL tubes. Add volume of NIB:HEPES to suspend 1M nuclei aliquots in 5 mL . We generally generate multiple replicates of the 1M nuclei aliquots. We have also done up to 3M nuclei aliquots without any change to protocol which performed the same.

steps.title: Prepare tagmentation plate Combine 420 µL,...
steps.instruction: Prepare tagmentation plate Combine 420 µL, 500 nuc/uL solution with 540 µL, 2X TD Buffer per 96 well iTSM plate. Pipette out 8uL per well of TD Buffer: Nuclei solution. Add 1uL of 2.5uM uniquely indexed SBS12-U-ME TSM per well. Final reagents per well in tagmentation plate. %s %s Total 9ul 20ng DNA 3.5 ul (~5k nuclei at a concentration of 1.4K nuclei/uL) TSM 1 uL 2.5uM Tn5 TDB1 4.5ul 2X TD Buffer Hold SBS12-U-ME TSM plate on ice while pipetting out. Also hold nuclei and tagmentation plate on ice until plate is fully prepared.

steps.title: Incubate at 55 °C for 00:10:00...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 55 °C for 00:10:00 while shaking at 300 rpm, °C, . Shaking helps keep nuclei suspended during tagmentation.

steps.title: Place plate on ice following incubation...
steps.instruction: Place plate on ice following incubation and hold for 00:02:00 to allow reactions to drop temperature.

steps.title: Pool the full plate while maintaining...
steps.instruction: Pool the full plate while maintaining plate and pool on ice. Add 2 µL, 5mg/mL DAPI to pool. Hold pool on ice while preparing the PCR plate. Nuclei are stable on ice for several hours. If there are any visible chunks in pool, strain again through 40um cell strainer.

steps.title: Prepare a 96 well plate with...
steps.instruction: Prepare a 96 well plate with 9 µL, 1X TD Buffer per well , diluted with ddH2O.

steps.title: Example gating strategy on DAPI+ nuclei...
steps.instruction: Example gating strategy on DAPI+ nuclei using the Sony SH800S Cell Sorter. From the DAPI stained pool, sort 15 nuclei per well mimicking the above nuclei gating strategy. If possible, hold the sample chamber and pool at 4 °C in your sorter settings. The number of nuclei per well for PCR is determined by the number of uniquely indexed transposases going into tagmentation. For 96 reactions (a single iTSM plate), it has been emperically deteremined to be 15 nuclei per well based on a 5% barnyard collision rate.

steps.title: Following sorting into the 96 well...
steps.instruction: Following sorting into the 96 well plate, centrifuge the plate at 500 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes to ensure nuclei are within the solution. SAFE STOPPING POINT: Plates can be frozen (stored at -20 °C )after spin down for at least 1 week without measurable decrease in library quality.

steps.title: Add 1 µL, 0
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL, 0.1% SDS to each well.

steps.title: Add 4 µL, NPM to each well
steps.instruction: Add 4 µL, NPM to each well. It is crucial to dd 0.1% SDS and NPM in step-wise fashion and not pre-combined. High SDS concentration will disrupt polymerases in NPM.

steps.title: Gap-fill molecules by incubating plate at 72 °C for 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Gap-fill molecules by incubating plate at 72 °C for 00:10:00 . After extension, hold plate On ice to slow reaction.

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.5 µL, A14-LNA_ME at 1uM to a final concentration of 100 nanomolar (nM)

steps.title: Use multiple rounds of linear extension...
steps.instruction: Use multiple rounds of linear extension to switch adapter on one side of the molecules. 98 °C for 00:00:30 98 °C for 00:00:10 59 °C for 00:00:20 72 °C for 00:00:10 Go to step 2 9 times for 10 total cycles. Hold at 10 °C

steps.title: Add 1 µL, 1% (v/v) Triton X-100
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL, 1% (v/v) Triton X-100 . It is important that the ratio is 10:1 TritonX:SDS to quench SDS prior to PCR polymerase use. SAFE STOPPING POINT: Plates can be frozen (stored at -20 °C )after spin down for at least 10 weeks without measurable decrease in library quality.

steps.title: Combine the following reagents for a...
steps.instruction: Combine the following reagents for a RT-PCR reaction directly to the 96 well plate contained the samples. ddH2O, Q5U Master Mix and 100X SYBR can be made as a mastermix prior to added to wells. %s %s PCR primers (S70x (TruSeq i7) and N50x (Nextera i5) we sent last time) to final conc. 500nM 2.5 uL each primer Sample 16.5 ul H2O 3 uL Q5U 25 ul 100X SYBR 0.5uL Total 50 ul for PCR

steps.title: Perform a real-time PCR , measuring SYBR fluorecence every cycle
steps.instruction: Perform a real-time PCR , measuring SYBR fluorecence every cycle. 98 °C for 00:00:30 98 °C for 00:00:10 55 °C for 00:00:20 72 °C for 00:00:30 Measure SYBR fluoresence. 72 °C for 00:00:10 Go to step 2 16 more times for 17 cycles total. Hold at 72 °C for 00:02:00 Hold at 10 °C PCR takes between 15-17 cycles. Use SYBR fluoresence to guide when to pull libraries. Once fluoresence stops exponential growth, the libraries are ready to hold at 72C and then remove. SAFE STOPPING POINT: Plates can be frozen (stored at -20 °C )after spin down for at least 10 weeks without measurable decrease in library quality. Can also be held at 4 °C for at least 5 weeks.

steps.title: Pool post-PCR Product Pool 25 uL...
steps.instruction: Pool post-PCR Product Pool 25 uL from each well into 15mL conical tube.

steps.title: Concentrate DNA via column clean up...
steps.instruction: Concentrate DNA via column clean up Run full pool volume through Qiaquick PCR purification column following manufacturer's protocol. Qiagen QIAquick PCR Purification Kit Protocol by Michael Crone Elute in 50 µL, 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0 .

steps.title: Clean by size selection with SPRI...
steps.instruction: Clean by size selection with SPRI beads Perform a 1X SPRI bead size selection (selecting for DNA > 200 bp).

steps.title: Add 50 µL, MagPure NGS Binding...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL, MagPure NGS Binding Beads to column elution, once beads are at room temperature. Ensure beads are fully mixed prior to taking from stock. Vortex for at least 10 seconds immediately before pipetting out. Let mixture incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Place tube on magnetic rack and...
steps.instruction: Place tube on magnetic rack and wait for magnetic beads to pellet and elution to fully clear (roughly 00:02:00 ). Remove full volume of elution without disrupting bead pellet. Discard elution.

steps.title: Resuspend bead pellet in freshly prepared...
steps.instruction: Resuspend bead pellet in freshly prepared 100 µL, 80% ethanol (v/v) . Remove full volume of elution without disrupting bead pellet. Discard ethanol.

steps.title: Resuspend bead pellet in freshly prepared...
steps.instruction: Resuspend bead pellet in freshly prepared 100 µL, 80% ethanol (v/v) . Remove full volume of elution without disrupting bead pellet. Discard ethanol.

steps.title: Remove tube from magnetic rack and...
steps.instruction: Remove tube from magnetic rack and spin briefly on tapletop centrifuge. Return to magnetic rack and once beads are pelleted again, remove any remaining ethanol from bottom of tube.

steps.title: Resuspend beads off magnetic rack in...
steps.instruction: Resuspend beads off magnetic rack in 31 µL, 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0 . Let mixture incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00 for DNA to fully become suspended. Place tube on magnetic rack and wait for magnetic beads to pellet and elution to fully clear (roughly 00:02:00 ). Remove full volume of elution without disrupting bead pellet and move elution to clean tube.

steps.title: Qubit DNA HS Quantification Quantify DNA...
steps.instruction: Qubit DNA HS Quantification Quantify DNA concentration with 1uL eluted sample on Qubit DNA High-sensitivity kit following manufacturer's protocol. Qubit dsDNA HS/BR Assay by Sarah Hessen-Schmidt

steps.title: Agilent Tapestation 4150 Quantification Dilute sample...
steps.instruction: Agilent Tapestation 4150 Quantification Dilute sample to 2 ng/uL based on read out of Qubit by addition of 10mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0. Run 2 uL sample on Agilent Tapestation 4150 DNA High Sensitivity D5000 or D1000 following manufacturer's protocol. Example Agilent Tapestation 4150 DNA High Sensitivity D5000 of s3WGS library.

steps.title: Use Tapestation software to quantify DNA from 200-1000bp size
steps.instruction: Use Tapestation software to quantify DNA from 200-1000bp size. Dilute library to 1nM and prepare for sequencing following Illumina protocols.

steps.title: Load libraries according to standard loading...
steps.instruction: Load libraries according to standard loading concentrations suggested by Illumina with the following cycle counts per read. Sequencing is standard chemistry. %s %s

steps.title: To the 5mL nuclei suspension, add...
steps.instruction: To the 5mL nuclei suspension, add 246 µL, 16% formaldehyde from a freshly opened ampule. 0.75% formaldehyde final concentration is ideal.

steps.title: Incubate nuclei at Room temperature for...
steps.instruction: Incubate nuclei at Room temperature for 00:10:00 on an orbital shaker set to 50 rpm, °C, .

steps.title: Centrifuge nuclei at 500 x g,...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge nuclei at 500 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes to pellet nuclei. Aspirate off solution, being careful not to disturb the pellet.

steps.title: Wash pellet with 1 mL NIB:Tris
steps.instruction: Wash pellet with 1 mL NIB:Tris . Tris will quench the formaldehyde Centrifuge nuclei at 500 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes to pellet nuclei. Aspirate off solution, being careful not to disturb the pellet.

steps.title: Wash pellet with 200 µL 1X NEBuffer 2
steps.instruction: Wash pellet with 200 µL 1X NEBuffer 2.1 (ice cold) CRITICAL: Buffer recipe listed above is 10X. Ensure you dilute to 1X before use. Transfer resuspended nucleito 1.5mL tube for improved pelleting. Centrifuge nuclei at 500 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes to pellet nuclei. Aspirate off solution, being careful not to disturb the pellet.

steps.title: Construct 5mL of 10X NEBuffer 2
steps.instruction: Construct 5mL of 10X NEBuffer 2.1 %s %s Concentration (10X) Stock Concentration Volume Pipetted (For 5mL of 10X) 500mM NaCl 5M NaCl 500 uL 100mM Tris-HCl 1M Tris HCl (pH 7.5) 500 uL 100mM MgCl2 1M MgCl2 500 uL 1ng/uL BSA 100ng/uL BSA 50 uL pH 7.9 @ 25C H2O 3450 uL CRITICAL : Ensure that this buffer is diluted to 1X before use.

steps.title: Resuspend pellet in 760 µL 1X NEBuffer 2
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet in 760 µL 1X NEBuffer 2.1

steps.title: Add 40 µL of 1% SDS...
steps.instruction: Add 40 µL of 1% SDS and incubate at 300 rpm, 37 °C, 20 minutes on a ThermoMixer 0.05% final concentration SDS is ideal

steps.title: Centrifuge nuclei at 500 x g,...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge nuclei at 500 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes to pellet nuclei. Aspirate off solution, being careful not to disturb the pellet.

steps.title: Resuspend pellet in 50 µL NIB:Tris
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet in 50 µL NIB:Tris. If there are multiple replicates they can be pooled at this point. If there is significant clumping, nuclei can bed loosed by a 10 min incubation in NIB:Tris and then triturated. If this does not work, loose strokes (B) on a douce homogenizer works as well.

steps.title: Take aliquot of sample and dilute...
steps.instruction: Take aliquot of sample and dilute (5uL of nuclei suspension in 45 µL, NIB:Tris for a 1:10 dilution).

steps.title: Determine nuclei/uL concentration of diluted aliquot...
steps.instruction: Determine nuclei/uL concentration of diluted aliquot through Hemocytometer or BioRad TC20 Automated Cell Counter using manufacturer's recommended protocols. Adjust calculated concentration for the stock nuclei suspension. Example of fixed and nucleosome depleted nuclei imaged on BioRad TC20. Note ensure that nuclei count accounts for the debris present and an accurate nuclear count is attained.

steps.title: Dilute nuclei to 500 nuclei/uL with...
steps.instruction: Dilute nuclei to 500 nuclei/uL with NIB:Tris for aliquoting out to tagmentation plate.
